Textile Engineering × AI

Bridging textile engineering with machine learning, computer vision, and robotics.

I build applied systems with a strong research spine: smart thermal garments, real-time textile inspection, and robotic fabric manipulation. The goal is simple: make the work rigorous enough for competitive graduate admissions and credible enough for top-tier engineering teams.

Download CVView Research
Textile EngineeringGrounded in real manufacturing constraints and materials behavior.
Data Science ReadyML, CV, control, and reproducible experimentation across the stack.
Research + BuildStructured to communicate both academic depth and engineering execution.
Profile

Tahmid Al Muntasir

Final-year engineering student building rigorous, deployment-minded systems at the intersection of textile engineering, machine learning, computer vision, and robotics.

Machine LearningComputer VisionRobotics

Current Focus
2025–PresentIn progress
Bio-based PCM thesis

Designing and validating the thermal regulation system, from simulation and control baselines to a deployable smart-garment prototype.

Industrial rotation IICompleted
Textile manufacturing rotation

Process observation, production-floor analysis, and the link between textile manufacturing constraints and automation opportunities.

Selected Projects

Professional showcase

View all projects ↗
P1Thesis Trackactive

AI-Driven Smart Thermal Garments

A wearable control stack that combines bio-based PCM, thermal sensing, and hybrid predictive control for adaptive comfort regulation.

GitHub ↗
PCMESP32PyTorchSciPyMPC
Bio-based PCM thesis in progressThermal model refinedEdge deployment path defined
P2Applied Visionactive

Real-Time CV for Fabric Defect Detection

A fast inspection pipeline for textile defects using YOLO-based detection and production-minded inference workflows.

GitHub ↗
YOLOPyTorchOpenCVRoboflowESP32
Dataset labeling underwayReal-time inference targetFactory deployment constraints mapped
P3Robotics Trackactive

RL for Robotic Fabric Manipulation

Simulation-first reinforcement learning for robot policies that can pick, align, and manipulate textile materials reliably.

GitHub ↗
ROSGazeboPyTorchRLComputer Vision
Simulation environment in progressPolicy design underwaySim-to-real path planned

Academic emphasis

Built for competitive Master’s in Data Science review and research-oriented engineering roles: a clean technical narrative, credible prototypes, and measurable progress.

Research Pillars

Current thesis architecture

Pillar 1Active — Thesis Track

AI-Driven Smart Thermal Garments

Bio-based PCM thermal regulation with hybrid control, edge deployment, and comfort-aware sensing.

Pillar 2Active — Applied Vision

Real-Time CV for Fabric Defect Detection

Production-ready defect detection for textile quality control using computer vision.

Pillar 3Active — Robotics Track

RL for Robotic Fabric Manipulation

Simulation-first reinforcement learning for textile handling and robot policy learning.
